abstract: This dissertation investigates behavioral, physiological, and transcriptomic variation among colonies of the red harvester ant (Pogonomyrmex barbatus). It integrates field behavioral ecology, neurochemistry, gene expression analysis, and theoretical frameworks to understand how colony-level collective behavior emerges from individual-level variation. The work examines the foraging gene, dopaminergic signaling, desiccation physiology, and RNA-Seq transcriptomics across multiple ant colonies in na
